channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: ‘Dead’ Tarkov Competitor Suddenly Breaks Two-Year Silence with New Plans
item.content:encoded:
Marauders, a space-based Escape from Tarkov competitor that was released into early access in October 2022, has emerged from the shadows after months of radio silence. For years, Small Impact Games, the developer behind this multiplayer extraction shooter, has relied on posting bland, repetitive content across social media, remaining quiet about any further evolution of the game.

As they sat in silence, the game dwindled, and at the time of writing, it had just 60 players on Steam, the only platform that Marauders calls home. But per the team at Small Impact, things are about to change. Marauders is coming back.

Tarkov in Space is Coming Back with an Overhaul

In October 2022, Marauders burst onto the extraction scene with a desire to challenge the status quo held by the likes of Escape from Tarkov. It was an ambitious move, and it generated a lot of attention, at least at first. The game pulled in 14,332 peak players on Steam and drew somewhat positive reviews across the board (but dropped recently).

Marauders had a unique premise. Players would kit up, hop onto a ship, and attempt to beat other players to floating rigs, space stations, and the like, docking and gaining entry through one of a few landing ports. From there, they’d fight AI enemies, other players, and the elements, all while seeking loot, fame, and fortune, before heading back to their ship to zip off into the safety of space.

It was great because you could be gunned down out of the inky blackness of space before you even made it to your destination.

What was better was the game’s theme. Marauders relies on a dieselpunk, dystopian universe that’s rooted in alternate history. It has a stellar aesthetic that was the crowning gem of the game’s make-up, bringing a retro-futuristic theme front and centre.

In a lengthy post on social media, Small Impact Games apologized for the radio silence and promised big things:

We owe you an apology for the silence.

We’ve been fully focused on the game. What started as a round of fixes grew into a much larger, necessary change and we have made the call to commit to it rather than push smaller updates in the meantime.

We’ve been reworking core systems and rebuilding mechanics that needed it. This isn’t a patch. It’s a full overhaul aimed at getting Marauders where it should be.

We don’t have a release date to share yet, and we’re not setting one until we’re confident we can hit it. What we can tell you is that the next update from us will be the Technical Stress Test branch opening for community testing.

To everyone who stuck with us, kept playing, and kept the community going. Thank you. We know it’s been a long wait, and we haven’t forgotten that.

Until now, Small Impact’s social presence had been confined to rinse-and-repeat posts with no meaningful content whatsoever.

On Steam, this was the team’s first official update since August 2024. Back then, the team revealed a major update, dubbed ‘Retribution’, and wiped the platform to ensure a level playing field for those still occupying the title. This is the first sizeable update the community, however small, has received in almost two years.

It’s not like Marauders is about to become the next Escape from Tarkov, but it’s good to see the game hasn’t yet died a full death.

Did you ever play Marauders? Let us know what you think about the game on the Insider Gaming Discord server.

For more Insider Gaming coverage, check out the news that Game Pass is getting a price decrease

Senior Editor and Esports Lead

Grant has been gaming for 30+ years and in the industry for 10+. You'll probably find him playing a post-apocalyptic game or an extraction shooter somewhere.

More from Grant Taylor-Hill

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Caedrel Offered to Come Out of Retirement to Represent Great Britain at ENC
item.content:encoded:
Former League of Legends pro-turned Twitch streamer Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont has been offered the chance to return to competitive play.

Later this year, the Esports World Cup Foundation is hosting the first-ever Esports Nations Cup, a $20 million tournament where mono-nation teams will compete across multiple esports events.

And popular streamer Marc ‘Caedrel’ Lamont has revealed he has been asked to represent Great Britain at the Esports Nations Cup.

He might want something to look forward to with the LoL co-streaming debate getting toxic.

Caedrel Reveals Team Great Britain Offer

Caedrel retired from pro play back in 2020 and hasn’t competed in the midlane since 2018 after roleswapping to the jungle.

Caedrel revealed Great Britain is making a “serious” team and admitted he was unsure on whether to accept the offer.

“But if I played mid, that basically means I have to go back to being pro, and I don’t know if that’s a good idea or not… I don’t know if I’ve got it in me.”

Caedrel stressed about having to learn mid champions like Aurora and what the grind would do for his health, saying:

“I feel like this would be the only chance I get to ever go back to pro, but I don’t know if I’ve got it in me.

“It might sound like a bad excuse, but my health gets pretty bad when I start grinding League because I get this obsessive personality that I play only. It ain’t good.”

However, Caedrel was also excited by the videos that could come through playing at the Esports Nations Cup:

“I could make cool content out of it, though. I could vlog, [make] path to pro, VOD review all my games on stream, get people to coach me on stream like Caps for lane phase.”

But then confessed his biggest fear: “I’m scared I’ll just run it down. What happens if I do all this build-up for months, and then I just get f***ing gapped?”

Caedrel also stressed that try-harding in NA solo queue while co-streaming Worlds just before the event would be a challenge.

What Could the Great Britain LoL ENC Roster Be?

Currently, there aren’t any British LoL players competing at a high level. None in the LEC, and there weren’t any at EMEA Masters 2026.

Caedrel said he couldn’t leak anything but claims Great Britain has a “tryhard” line-up and says the coach is quite a popular one.

Most of the UK’s talent is currently in coaching, and Team Vitality’s head of coaching development and former head coach James ‘Mac’ MacCormack is reported to be leading the Great Britain LoL team.

It has been four years since a British pro played in the LEC, after toplaner Barney ‘Alphari’ Morris and bot-laner Matthew ‘xMatty’ Coombs competed in 2024.

Alphari is now coaching in the LEC, as is former Misfits Gaming jungler Nubar ‘Maxlore’ Sarafian, and it’s possible that both could come out of retirement.

xMatty could be the bot lane choice, though the more likely choice is Matthew ‘Deadly’ Smith, who has been playing in the very competitive LFL with Skillcamp.

And the support role will likely go to another veteran and former LEC player in Raymond ‘Kasing’ Tsang.

Great Britain could look to the ERLs for emerging talents too, though the nation’s NLC is currently suspended with no tournament organiser.

What roster would you want to see from Great Britain for the Esports Nations Cup? Let us know on the Insider Gaming Discord.

And see the 100 partnered organisations representing countries at the Esports Nations Cup.

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Answering Your Questions About Lacrosse 26
item.content:encoded:
Lacrosse 26 is launching on April 24, and there are still plenty of unknowns about the first lacrosse video game to hit consoles since Casey Powell Lacrosse 18. The game was first announced as somewhat of a surprise, a little over a week ago, and details on gameplay and franchise mode have come out.

Still, fans have plenty of questions regarding what to expect in Lacrosse 26. I’ve been able to get early access to the game, and with permission from the developers at Crosse Studios, I’m here to answer some of those questions for you.

Lacrosse 26 – What you wanted to know

What is in the game at launch?

On release, Lacrosse 26 features quick play, practice, and franchise modes.

Is it cross-platform?

Lacrosse 26 doesn’t feature any online play. It does, however, have local multiplayer with up to four players.

What’s the price?

$34.99

How deep is the team creator?

This has been the most common question about the game. I can tell you that it’s not as deep as what Casey Powell Lacrosse 18 had. That was a creation system added to a game that already had a foundation. With Lacrosse 26 being built from the ground up, the developers tell me that more of the focus went into the depth of franchise mode and the gameplay.

Team creator has you select from one of a few dozen team names, such as Buffalo, Albany, Eagles, Greyhounds, and more. There are five stadiums, 99 logos, and 10 jersey templates to choose from. You can also edit your roster, but only portraits and names are available at launch. Further player customization is planned for a future update.

Can you create a team for quick play?

No. Team creator is for franchise mode only.

Will we be able to upload our own images for team creation?

No. The game instead has 99 logos to choose from for your team.

How’s it for specialist positions?

Positions in the game are standard: Attack, Midfielder, Defense, Goalie.

How many teams are in franchise mode?

There are 71 teams total across 10 conferences. It’s also worth noting that there is no official licensing, but you can tell which programs the in-game teams are based on.

What can you do in franchise mode?

Obviously, you play your games—note, there’s no simulation option. You’re playing the full 18-game season! You’ll also be able to recruit players, upgrade things like team morale, fan happiness, team fitness, and overall hype that give you various bonuses.

What is lineup customization like?

In franchise and quick play, you can actually set two separate lineups per position. Within games, you can quickly swap to them using the d-pad.

What is the gameplay like?

I’ll have more in my full impressions piece, but it has its hits and misses. The general player movement and shooting feel solid; Passing and defensive play could use some improvement. You can set attack formations, but it doesn’t appear that you can do the same on defense.

How do player ratings and attributes work?

From my short time with the game, they work like almost every other sports game. It helps determine the skill of the CPU team you play against, as well as the abilities of your team. You can definitely tell the difference when playing as a team with good shooters compared to one without.

What camera angles are there?

I was surprised to see the number of camera angles available. You can choose from: Low, Medium, High, Nosebleeds, Low Dynamic, Side, and Broadcast.

Are you going to pick up Lacrosse 26 on April 24 for PlayStation 5 or Xbox Series X|S? Let us know down in the comments and in the official Insider Gaming Discord.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

MOBILE APP

item.dc:creator: invalid@example.com (ResetEra_Newsbot

item.title: Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Combat ‘Reworked To An Action-Oriented Experience’
item.content:encoded:
There’s a distinct line in the sand when it comes to Assassin’s Creed combat: Those who stand on the side of the classic combat, and newcomers who’ve become familiar with the more RPG/action-heavy combat of newer games. A Ubisoft leak highlights that Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced’s combat looks to lean into action with new changes.

For years, AC combat was fairly basic, but satisfying, as you became adept at dodging and countering, all the while making use of time-period-specific gadgets and your hidden blade. Recent Assassin’s Creed games have gone off the deep end and gone full RPG—I’m looking at you in Assassin’s Creed Origins, Odyssey, and Valhalla.

Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced will also utilize action elements.

Combat To Be Action-Heavy in Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced?

Ah, the classic Assassin’s Creed Black Flag HUD in the top-right corner with the likes of ‘shoot,’ ‘counter,’ ‘break defense.’

They were simpler times indeed. Times change, though, and as we’ve come to learn with Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced, the combat will be updated, just like the modern-day Animus has, and as have tailing/eavesdropping missions.

Through a leaked Resynced presentation, we’ve been able to gather key details about the remake, including Edward Kenway’s original voice actor returning for the role.

While we can’t share physical footage of the presentation, we can tell you about the combat, courtesy of Matt Ryan himself:

“The combat system has been entirely reworked into an action-oriented experience. While wielding dual swords, pistols, and hidden blades, every move now plays a specific role with faster and more fluid attacks and combos. Land a perfect parry to open up your target for an instant kill, then follow with up to four chained takedowns.

Use walls, ledges, and breakables to your advantage. Keep track of your opponents and use all your tools to level the playing field.”

Combat sees a double-edged HUD, with two mini-menus in each bottom corner, allowing you to quickly cycle between weapons and items, and it does look like an intrinsic addition to skirmishes. If you did a side-by-side of Resynced and Black Flag, you would quickly notice how much faster Resynced is, with an emphasis on grander and more fluid fights.

It’s a far cry (no, not that one) from the original Black Flag. The gameplay footage shows obvious visual cues for enemy attacks, with different-coloured flourishes to signify the type of attack and threat level, i.e., unblockable attacks. Time will tell if long-time fans grow to like it, or if it’ll be cast into Davy Jones’ locker.

It won’t be long until you get to see the combat in action for yourself. Once you do, let us know what you think via the free-to-join and always lively Insider Gaming Discord.

We have more Resynced content from the Assassin’s Creed Black Flag Resynced Collector’s Edition goodies being revealed, to the news that Resynced doesn’t include DLC or Multiplayer.

Written by

Evergreen Editor

Andy is Insider Gaming's Senior Evergreen Editor and has been in the games journalism sphere in one way or another for over 7 years. His video game taste is as…

More from Andrew Highton
